title: Processes and Scheduling
description: Covers processes, the PCB, scheduling algorithms, fork, and IPC.

questions.prompt: What is the term for an active entity: a program in execution, loaded into memory?
Which data structure holds information about a process (state, PC, registers, scheduling info, memory info)?
Match each scheduler performance metric to its definition.
right: Fraction of CPU busy when jobs are in the ready queue
End time minus arrival time minus burst time
Number of processes completed per unit time

questions.prompt: Which of the following statements about non-preemptive scheduling are TRUE?
options: Once a process is given the CPU, it holds it until the current CPU burst finishes
A running process can be interrupted to schedule another process
Typically simpler to implement than preemptive scheduling

questions.prompt: Which scheduler controls the number of processes in memory by moving processes from the new state to ready?
Match each scheduling algorithm to its behaviour.
right: Switches to a newly-arrived process if it has a shorter next burst than the running one
Each process gets a fixed time quantum, then yields
Runs processes in order of arrival
Picks the process with the shortest predicted next CPU burst; the running one finishes first

questions.prompt: A child process that has terminated but whose exit status has not yet been collected by its parent is called what?
Which of the following statements about inter-process communication are TRUE?
options: Shared memory is typically faster than message passing because it has no syscall overhead
Message passing uses a kernel-provided communication channel
Named pipes persist in the file system after the processes using them exit
Pipes provide two-way communication by default
Blocking send blocks the sender until the message is received
